CHECK_TITLE: Ensure hardware MFA is enabled for the root account
STATUS_EXTENDED: Root account does not have hardware MFA enabled
DESCRIPTION: The root account is the most privileged account in AWS. Hardware MFA adds an extra layer of protection by requiring physical device authentication in addition to the password.
RISK: Without hardware MFA, the root account is more susceptible to compromise through credential theft or phishing attacks, potentially allowing attackers complete control of the AWS environment.
REMEDIATION_RECOMMENDATION_TEXT: Enable hardware MFA for the root account by navigating to IAM console > Security Credentials > Assign MFA device, selecting a hardware MFA device, and completing the setup wizard.

CHECK_TITLE: Ensure IAM password policy requires minimum length of 14 or greater
STATUS_EXTENDED: Password policy minimum length is 8 which is less than 14
DESCRIPTION: AWS IAM password policies can be used to ensure that passwords are at least a given length. Passwords of at least 14 characters are strongly recommended.
RISK: Weak password policies increase the risk of brute-force attacks or password guessing, enabling unauthorised access to AWS accounts and resources.
REMEDIATION_RECOMMENDATION_TEXT: Update the account password policy to require a minimum length of 14 characters. Navigate to IAM > Account Settings > Password Policy and set the minimum password length to 14.

CHECK_TITLE: Ensure no root account access key exists
STATUS_EXTENDED: Root account has active access keys
DESCRIPTION: The root account is the most privileged AWS account. Root access keys should not exist as they cannot be restricted and provide unrestricted access to all AWS services.
RISK: Active root access keys represent a critical risk as they cannot be scoped to specific permissions. If compromised, an attacker would have full, unrestricted access to all AWS resources.
REMEDIATION_RECOMMENDATION_TEXT: Delete all root account access keys. Log in to the AWS Management Console as root, navigate to IAM > Security Credentials, and delete any listed access keys.

CHECK_TITLE: Ensure MFA is enabled for all IAM users that have a console password
STATUS_EXTENDED: IAM user alice has console access but MFA not enabled
DESCRIPTION: Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) adds an extra layer of protection on top of a username and password. With MFA enabled, when a user signs in, they are required to provide both their password and a one-time code from their MFA device.
RISK: Without MFA, compromised IAM user credentials could allow unauthorised access to the AWS console and associated resources.
REMEDIATION_RECOMMENDATION_TEXT: Enable MFA for all IAM users with console access. Navigate to IAM > Users, select each user, go to Security Credentials, and assign an MFA device.

CHECK_TITLE: Ensure S3 bucket has all four S3 Block Public Access settings enabled
STATUS_EXTENDED: S3 bucket my-public-bucket does not have all Block Public Access settings enabled
DESCRIPTION: Amazon S3 Block Public Access provides settings for access points, buckets, and accounts to help manage public access to Amazon S3 resources.
RISK: Publicly accessible S3 buckets may expose sensitive data to unauthorised users, leading to data leakage or compliance violations.
REMEDIATION_RECOMMENDATION_TEXT: Enable all four S3 Block Public Access settings on the bucket. Navigate to S3 > Bucket > Permissions > Block Public Access settings, and enable all four settings.

CHECK_TITLE: Ensure all S3 buckets employ encryption-at-rest
STATUS_EXTENDED: S3 bucket my-unencrypted-bucket does not have server-side encryption enabled
DESCRIPTION: Server-side encryption (SSE) is about data encryption at rest, i.e. Amazon S3 encrypts your data at the object level as it writes it to disks in its data centres and decrypts it for you when you access it.
RISK: Unencrypted data at rest is vulnerable to physical access attacks and could result in data breaches if storage media is improperly disposed of or accessed.
REMEDIATION_RECOMMENDATION_TEXT: Enable server-side encryption on the S3 bucket. Navigate to S3 > Bucket > Properties > Default Encryption, and enable SSE-S3 or SSE-KMS.

CHECK_TITLE: Ensure MFA Delete is enabled on S3 buckets
STATUS_EXTENDED: S3 bucket my-data-bucket does not have MFA Delete enabled
DESCRIPTION: MFA Delete requires additional authentication for either of the following operations: changing the versioning state of your bucket, or permanently deleting an object version.
RISK: Without MFA Delete, an attacker with compromised credentials could permanently delete versioned objects, leading to data loss.
REMEDIATION_RECOMMENDATION_TEXT: Enable MFA Delete on versioned S3 buckets using the bucket owner's root credentials and an MFA device.

CHECK_TITLE: Ensure no security groups allow ingress from 0.0.0.0/0 to all ports
STATUS_EXTENDED: Security group sg-12345 (allow-all-inbound) allows unrestricted inbound access on all ports
DESCRIPTION: Security groups are stateful firewalls that control inbound and outbound traffic to AWS resources. Allowing unrestricted access from the internet to all ports exposes resources to scanning and exploitation.
RISK: Unrestricted inbound access exposes instances to brute-force attacks, port scanning, and exploitation of any vulnerable services running on the instances.
REMEDIATION_RECOMMENDATION_TEXT: Remove inbound rules that allow unrestricted access (0.0.0.0/0 or ::/0) on all ports. Apply the principle of least privilege — only allow traffic from known sources on required ports.

CHECK_TITLE: Ensure Instance Metadata Service Version 2 (IMDSv2) is enabled
STATUS_EXTENDED: EC2 instance i-0123456789abcdef0 does not have IMDSv2 enforced
DESCRIPTION: IMDSv2 uses session-oriented requests to prevent SSRF attacks from accessing the instance metadata service and obtaining sensitive instance credentials.
RISK: Without IMDSv2, SSRF vulnerabilities in applications running on the instance could allow attackers to retrieve IAM credentials from the metadata service.
REMEDIATION_RECOMMENDATION_TEXT: Enforce IMDSv2 on all EC2 instances. Use the AWS CLI to modify instance metadata options and set --http-tokens to required.

CHECK_TITLE: Ensure all data in Amazon EBS is encrypted
STATUS_EXTENDED: EBS volume vol-0123456789abcdef0 is not encrypted
DESCRIPTION: Elastic Block Store (EBS) volumes should be encrypted at rest to protect sensitive data stored on the volumes.
RISK: Unencrypted EBS volumes could expose sensitive data if the volume is accessed by an unauthorised party or if the underlying storage media is compromised.
REMEDIATION_RECOMMENDATION_TEXT: Enable EBS volume encryption. Create a new encrypted volume, copy the data, and replace the unencrypted volume. Enable EBS encryption by default in the EC2 console.

CHECK_TITLE: Ensure CloudTrail is enabled in all regions
DESCRIPTION: AWS CloudTrail records AWS API calls for your account and delivers log files to you. A multi-region trail ensures that activity in all regions is captured.
RISK: Without multi-region CloudTrail, malicious activity in non-primary regions would go undetected, enabling attackers to operate without audit trails.
REMEDIATION_RECOMMENDATION_TEXT: Create a multi-region CloudTrail trail or update the existing trail to cover all regions. Enable log file validation to detect tampering.

CHECK_TITLE: Ensure CloudTrail log file validation is enabled
STATUS_EXTENDED: CloudTrail trail prod-trail does not have log file validation enabled
DESCRIPTION: Enabling log file validation generates a hash that allows detection of whether CloudTrail log files were modified, deleted, or unchanged after delivery to S3.
RISK: Without log file validation, an attacker who gains access to the S3 bucket containing CloudTrail logs could tamper with or delete evidence of their activity.
REMEDIATION_RECOMMENDATION_TEXT: Enable log file validation on all CloudTrail trails to ensure log integrity.

CHECK_TITLE: Ensure RDS instances have storage encryption enabled
STATUS_EXTENDED: RDS instance db-prod01 does not have storage encryption enabled
DESCRIPTION: Amazon RDS encrypted instances use industry-standard AES-256 encryption. RDS encryption protects data at rest including automated backups, read replicas, and snapshots.
RISK: Unencrypted RDS instances expose database contents to risk if the underlying storage is compromised, potentially resulting in sensitive data exposure.
REMEDIATION_RECOMMENDATION_TEXT: Enable storage encryption on RDS instances. Note that encryption cannot be enabled on an existing unencrypted instance — create a new encrypted instance and migrate the data.

CHECK_TITLE: Ensure rotation for customer-created symmetric CMKs is enabled
STATUS_EXTENDED: KMS CMK key-12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012 does not have rotation enabled
DESCRIPTION: Rotating encryption keys helps reduce the potential impact of a compromised key. KMS key rotation annually replaces the key material while retaining the logical key resource.
RISK: Without key rotation, a compromised KMS key remains effective indefinitely, potentially exposing all data encrypted with that key.
REMEDIATION_RECOMMENDATION_TEXT: Enable automatic key rotation for all customer-managed CMKs in KMS.

CHECK_TITLE: Ensure VPC flow logging is enabled in all VPCs
STATUS_EXTENDED: VPC vpc-12345678 does not have flow logs enabled
DESCRIPTION: VPC Flow Logs capture information about IP traffic going to and from network interfaces in your VPC. This data can be used to monitor traffic, detect security anomalies, and investigate incidents.
RISK: Without VPC flow logs, network-level intrusions and exfiltration activities may go undetected, limiting incident response capabilities.
REMEDIATION_RECOMMENDATION_TEXT: Enable VPC flow logs for all VPCs. Logs should be sent to CloudWatch Logs or S3 for centralised analysis and alerting.
REMEDIATION_CODE_CLI: aws ec2 create-flow-logs --resource-type VPC --resource-ids vpc-12345678 --traffic-type ALL --log-destination-type cloud-watch-logs --log-group-name /aws/vpc/flowlogs

CHECK_TITLE: Ensure GuardDuty is enabled in all regions
STATUS_EXTENDED: GuardDuty is not enabled in region us-east-1
DESCRIPTION: Amazon GuardDuty is a threat detection service that continuously monitors for malicious activity and unauthorised behaviour to protect AWS accounts and workloads.
RISK: Without GuardDuty, there is no automated mechanism to detect threats such as compromised instances, unauthorised access attempts, or data exfiltration.
REMEDIATION_RECOMMENDATION_TEXT: Enable Amazon GuardDuty in all AWS regions used by the organisation, including regions where services are not actively deployed, to detect cross-region attacks.
